description: A Solar Energy Systems Engineer needs to evaluate the performance difference between Premium Crystalline Silicon and Thin Film PV modules in the extreme hot climate of Phoenix, AZ. Thin film modules typically have a lower temperature coefficient, meaning they suffer less efficiency loss in high heat. Using PySAM (`Pvwattsv8`), model a 50 MW DC tracking PV plant using the Phoenix weather data at `/home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv`. Configure both systems with 1-Axis Tracking, a DC-to-AC ratio of 1.25, inverter efficiency of 96.0%, system losses of 14.08%, a Ground Coverage Ratio (GCR) of 0.35, 0 tilt, and 180 azimuth. Compare a Premium Crystalline Silicon system (`module_type` 1) against a Thin Film system (`module_type` 2). Determine the annual energy, summer energy (June through August), and winter energy (December through February) for both module types, as well as the seasonal percentage gain of Thin Film over Premium. Save all results to `/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/temp_coef_comparison.json` using the keys: `premium_annual_energy_kwh`, `thinfilm_annual_energy_kwh`, `premium_summer_energy_kwh`, `thinfilm_summer_energy_kwh`, `premium_winter_energy_kwh`, `thinfilm_winter_energy_kwh`, `thinfilm_summer_gain_percent`, and `thinfilm_winter_gain_percent`.
